scope: legible resistor values on retained native electrical sheets and registered .009 target-board bodies
promotion_rule: promote only a value printed unambiguously beside the same refdes, on the factory-identified target body, or in a clearly bracketed identical group; do not infer values from topology or color similarity

groups.name: sheet-1 decode open-collector pullups
groups.observation: R11/R12 are printed 1k above D6 outputs; R13/R14 are printed 1k beside the D13 input branches

groups.name: sheet-1 decode RC series resistor
groups.observation: R17 is printed 200 beside C99=160 in the D7/D9 decode path

groups.name: sheet-2 S3 switch pullup bank
groups.observation: the six identical R40-R45 pullups share the printed 15k group value below R45; the former 13k prose was a transcription error

groups.name: sheet-2 D56 timing network
groups.observation: the two AG3 timing resistors are printed R47=20k and R59=33k; the clear pullup is printed R61=12k

groups.name: sheet-2 video summing stage
groups.observation: each value is printed directly beside its refdes around VT2; the .006 R67 value is not reused because the target body is independently readable

groups.name: .009 target video-clamp body
groups.observation: the factory drawing fixes the red vertical body between R66 and VD3 as R67; independent July and May owner views both directly read 4K7, superseding the .006 sheet's 2k for this target-revision part without assigning its unresolved far endpoint

groups.name: sheet-2 beeper clamp
groups.observation: R90=2k and R91=1k are printed on the D58/VD4/VT1 AVDC branch; R48 is printed 8,2 Ом on the VT1 emitter-to-speaker output and the independently traced beeper boundary records the same 8.2R value

held.reason: Recovered .009 Э3 sheet 3 proves the D106 preset/UP pull-up role and +5 V endpoint, but its value marking and physical body are not readable in the available source set.
